title: pittosporum inopinatum
intro: Pittosporum inopinatum, also known as Tī Kōuka, is a native New Zealand tree with a rich history in traditional Māori medicine, valued for its unique properties and versatility. The plant's bark and leaves have been used in traditional medicine for their antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties, which aid in treating various health issues. The medicinal properties of Pittosporum inopinatum have been found to provide health benefits such as reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and supporting immune function. The plant can be prepared through infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, making it a valuable resource in herbal medicine.
